subject: [PATCH v4 8/8] mm: switch deferred split shrinker to list_lru

reviews.author: Usama Arif
reviews.summary:
The reviewer noted that deferred_split_folio() can set the shrinker bit on the wrong memcg if the original memcg is dying and already reparented, and suggested that __list_lru_add() should use the parent memcg instead of the original one.

The reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where an allocation fails and suggested setting the result to SCAN_ALLOC_HUGE_PAGE_FAIL.
reviews.raw_body:
Can the shrinker bit end up on the wrong memcg here?

deferred_split_folio() takes the lock via list_lru_lock_irqsave() with
the original folio_memcg() of the folio. If that memcg is dying and
already reparented, lock_list_lru_of_memcg() walks up parent_mem_cgroup()
until it finds a live sublist and locks it (lru), but the memcg local
variable still points at the dying child.

__list_lru_add() then calls set_shrinker_bit(memcg, ...) with the
original (dying / reparented) memcg, not the parent that actually owns
the locked sublist where the folio was queued.

---

Over here, you will end up reporting success on allocation failure.

Maybe set result to SCAN_ALLOC_HUGE_PAGE_FAIL?

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that there is an existing issue with list_lru, agreed to fix it first and then rebase their patches

Author acknowledged a concern about the deferred split queue's locking behavior and promised to address it in a follow-up fix.
reviews.raw_body:
I think you're right. Good catch.

This looks like an existing list_lru issue, actually. Even before,
list_lru_add() would call lock_list_lru_of_memcg() which might do the
hierarchy walk. But then set_shrinker_bit() on the passed in memcg.

I'll fix this in list_lru first. It's a likely backport
candidate. Then rebase my patches on top.

---

Good point. Sashiko pointed that out as well. I have a follow-up fix.

Thanks for taking a look.

2026-05-26.patch_summary: This patch switches the deferred split shrinker to use list_lru, which properly sets up per-node and per-cgroup lists. This improves handling of cgroups in a suboptimal fashion by avoiding an imperfect guard rail that splits large pages on other nodes when a node-restricted allocation enters reclaim. The change also streamlines list operations and reclaim walks, and is used widely by other major shrinkers.
